LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE AUG-08-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JANE DOE'S MOTION TO PROCEED PSEUDONYMOUSLY, AND FOR ENTRY OF A PROTECTIVE ORDER, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. TRANSFERRED TO THE DISCOVERY CALENDAR TO BE HEARD ON AUGUST 28, 2024, AT 9:00AM. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JANE DOE'S MOTION TO PROCEED PSEUDONYMOUSLY, AND FOR ENTRY OF A PROTECTIVE ORDER. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE COURT HAS RECEIVED THE PARTIES' SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS. AFTER REVIEWING THEM, THE COURT ADOPTS THE JUDGE PRO TEM'S REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION DATED OCTOBER 4, 2024, IN WHICH THE PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO PROCEED PSEUDONYMOUSLY IS DENIED, AND THE PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED. THE PARTIES ARE FURTHER ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER ON A PROTECTIVE ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. CRC 3.1312 COMPLIANCE IS REQUIRED. JUDGE: ROCHELLE C. EAST. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. NOT REPORTED. BAILIFF: C. LOKE. (301/RCE)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Proceed Pseudonymously, And For Entry Of A Protective Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Hearing re: PLAINTIFF JANE DOE'S Motion To Proceed Pseudonymously, And For Entry Of A Protective Order.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

The court has received the parties' supplemental briefs. After reviewing them, the court adopts the judge pro tem's Report and Recommendation dated October 4, 2024, in which the Plaintiff's Motion to Proceed Pseudonymously is denied, and the Plaintiff's Motion for a Protective Order is granted. The parties are further ordered to meet and confer on a protective order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. CRC 3.1312 compliance is required.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: C. Loke. (301/RCE)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-31-2024 Set For Trial On Oct-20-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Proceed Pseudonymously, And For Entry Of A Protective Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-11-2024 Continued To Nov-22-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. HILARY HAMMELL, Esq. of Law Offices of Hilary Hammell, APC (tel: 510-250-3175) for Plaintiff Jane Doe.
Atty. AMY FRENZEN, Esq. of San Francisco City Attorney's Office (tel: 415-554-3885) for Defendant City And County of San Francisco.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF JANE DOE's Motion To Proceed Pseudonymously, And For Entry Of A Protective Order.

Matter argued. The Court requests supplemental briefing, no longer than 7 pages and to be filed by no later than close of business on November 14, 2024. Plainitff may submit a declaration. The parties are ordered to comply with local rule 2.7(b) and provide paper courtesy copies of their supplemental brief to department 302. The Court continues the matter to November 22, 2024.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR# 13714 (tel: 415-601-7054 / email: apourtabib@gmail.com). (302/RCE)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Proceed Pseudonymously, And For Entry Of A Protective Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Sep-18-2024 Continued To Oct-11-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF JANE DOE'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, And Motion To Proceed Pseudonymously, And For Entry Of A Protective Order.

Appearances (in court):

Hilary P. Hammell, Esq., Law Offices of, (510) 250-3175, for Plaintiff.

Amy P. Frenzen, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, (415) 554-3885, for Defendant.

There is no stipulation to the Judge Pro Tem deciding this matter.

At the outset, the Judge Pro Tem presents a disclosure to counsel and asks counsel to consider it. After consideration, there is no objection from either counsel and the matter proceeds.

The subject motion is discussed. At the conclusion of discussion, the subject motion is continued to Law & Motion on Oct-11-2024 in Department 302 at 9:30 a.m. The Judge Pro Tem is to prepare and submit a report and recommendation.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti. Clerk: R. Woods. Court Reporter: Emily Husari, CSR: 12148, T: (707) 799-5993, E: calicsr@yahoo.com. (525/JPT)
